title: Invisible Character: Copy & Paste Blank Space & Invisible Text
meta_description: Copy an invisible character or blank space — zero width space, Hangul filler, braille blank — for empty names and bios. Click any symbol to copy it instantly.
hero_tagline: Every invisible character and blank space Unicode offers — zero width spaces, fillers, and empty braille. Click any symbol to copy it instantly.
intro: An invisible character is a real Unicode character that renders as nothing — a blank space you can copy and paste where platforms normally reject empty input. People use them to make an invisible Discord name, add blank lines to an Instagram bio or caption, send a seemingly empty WhatsApp message, or create clean spacing in gaming nicknames. Every button below holds a genuine invisible or blank character: the tiles look empty, but the labels tell you exactly which character you are copying.

sections.label: Zero Width
sections.intro: These characters occupy no visual width at all — ideal for invisible usernames and for breaking up text without showing anything.

sections.label: Blank Spaces
sections.intro: Visible-width but empty: these spaces survive in places where the regular space bar character gets trimmed or collapsed.

sections.label: Wide Spaces
sections.intro: Extra-wide blank characters, including the full-width ideographic space used in CJK typography — great for big visible gaps.

sections.label: Fillers
sections.intro: Letters and patterns that render as empty glyphs — the braille blank and Hangul filler are the go-to picks for invisible game names.

cta: Use UltraTextGen to convert plain text into bold, italic, cursive, and 100+ other Unicode font styles — free and instant.
